title: asplenium adiantum-nigrum subsp. woronowii
intro: Asplenium adiantum-nigrum subsp. woronowii, also known as the black spleenwort fern, is a type of vascular plant in the Aspleniaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Asplenium adiantum-nigrum subsp. woronowii include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory conditions, and acting as a natural antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions that can be consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
